# LLM cassettes

Recorded SSE / JSON responses replayed by `tests/llm_mock.py` so the M2
integration suite is deterministic and offline.

## File format

- `<hash>.sse` — bytes the mock replays for a streaming
  `POST /v1/chat/completions`. Each cassette is split on the SSE record
  terminator (`\n\n`) and the records are emitted as separate HTTP body
  chunks so the OpenAI SDK observes them as discrete events over time.
- `<hash>.json` — bytes the mock replays for a non-streaming
  `POST /v1/chat/completions` (the title-helper endpoint). Returned
  with `Content-Type: application/json`.

## Hash computation

The cassette filename stem is the first 16 hex characters of the
SHA-256 of the canonical JSON form of the request body's
`{"messages","model","temperature"}` triple:

```python
import hashlib, json

def compute_cassette_hash(*, model: str, messages: list[dict], temperature: float | None = None) -> str:
    canonical = json.dumps(
        {"model": model, "messages": messages, "temperature": temperature},
        sort_keys=True,
        separators=(",", ":"),
    )
    return hashlib.sha256(canonical.encode()).hexdigest()[:16]
```

`tests/llm_mock.py` exports the same function as
`compute_cassette_hash(...)` for tests to derive the cassette filename
their request will hit.

The provider's `params.system` is folded into `messages[0]` by
`OpenAICompatibleProvider.stream` before the HTTP call, so it does NOT
appear separately in the hash — only the `messages` array as it goes
out on the wire matters.

## Delay markers

Cassette files may contain comment-only frames of the literal shape

```
: delay <seconds>
```

The mock's replay loop (in `_replay_sse`) interprets these as a
`asyncio.sleep` directive and DOES NOT forward them to the client.
This lets cassettes for the timeout / cancel-mid-stream tests encode
the timing of their stream without the mock needing per-test
configuration.

## Recording mode (deferred)

The M2 dispatch deferred the `LLM_RECORD=1` proxy-and-record path. The
six cassettes here were hand-crafted to match the SSE wire format the
OpenAI SDK expects. When recording is implemented, it will:

1. Detect `LLM_RECORD=1` at app startup.
2. Proxy any cassette-miss request to the real
   `AGENT_GATEWAY_BASE_URL` upstream.
3. Stream the response back to the SDK while writing it to disk under
   the computed hash.
4. On the next test run (without `LLM_RECORD`), the cassette is
   replayed.

## Inventory

| Filename               | Scenario                                | Consumers                                                                                   |
| ---------------------- | --------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `2d608ece902879ff.sse` | "hello" → 5 deltas + usage + done       | `test_streaming.py::test_post_message_streams_start_delta_usage_done` and friends           |
| `3dd4bd9c83f2ab9a.sse` | "trigger error" → 3 deltas then `error` | `test_streaming.py::test_provider_error_mid_stream_emits_terminal_error_frame_and_persists` |
| `4acfc6b0c6329570.sse` | "slow reply" → delta + 5s delay + delta | `test_streaming.py::test_timeout_persists_partial_and_emits_timeout_frame`                  |
| `5ddf73264ec8b887.sse` | "please write a long reply" → 40 spaced | `test_streaming.py::test_client_disconnect_persists_cancelled_partial`                      |
| `39ccf63a95ee42f7.sse` | "please cancel me" → 40 spaced          | `test_streaming.py::test_explicit_cancel_endpoint_204_and_persists_cancelled`               |
| `96231776c87e0fbe.sse` | branch reply (3-msg context)            | `test_streaming.py::test_post_message_branches_off_parent_id_when_provided`                 |
| `3ca44c87232b7da0.sse` | 256 × 1 KiB deltas → trips 1 MiB cap    | `test_streaming.py::test_history_cap_during_streaming_emits_history_too_large_error_frame`  |

Refreshing a cassette is a deliberate PR — never auto-regenerated by CI.
